1203	// executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
1204	// inside <instructions> when running against a harness that carries its own
1205	// rich system prompt (claude, codex, opencode). These harnesses already know
1206	// how to read files, run tools, and manage sessions — this text adds only the
1207	// DDx-specific execution contract.
1208	const executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText = `You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.
1209	
1210	## Step 0: size check (do this first)
1211	
1212	Before writing any code, decide whether this bead is sized for a single bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals that the bead is too big:
1213	
1214	- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
1215	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables rather than a single coherent change.
1216	- Description explicitly lists multiple feature-sized pieces ("AC4 is a tier-inference engine", "AC5 introduces a new status type").
1217	- You estimate more than ~500 lines of meaningful code change across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
1218	
1219	If the bead is too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:
1220	
1221	1. Use the ` + "`bash`" + ` tool to run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` to file child beads, one per coherent slice of work. Pass each child a focused title, description, and acceptance criteria; copy labels and ` + "`spec-id`" + ` from the parent.
1222	2. Use ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record the dependency edge.
1223	3. Append a short note to the parent (` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `) so future runs see why no implementation landed.
1224	4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` explaining the decomposition: list each child bead ID and the slice it covers, and stop.
1225	
1226	A clean decomposition that produces tractable children is a successful attempt. Do not push partial implementation alongside decomposition.
1227	
1228	If the bead IS sized for one attempt, continue with the rest of these instructions.
1229	
1230	## How to work
1231	
1232	1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.
1233	
1234	2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.
1235	
1236	3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically ` + "`cargo test`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` or ` + "`npm test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.
1237	
1238	4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.
1239	
1240	5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.
1241	
1242	## The bead contract overrides project defaults
1243	
1244	The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.
1245	
1246	## Quality bar and the review step
1247	
1248	After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**
1249	
1250	If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.
1251	
1252	## Constraints
1253	
1254	- Work only inside this execution worktree.
1255	- Keep ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + ` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
1256	- **Never run ` + "`ddx init`" + `** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
1257	- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
1258	- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
1259	
1260	## When the work is done
1261	
1262	After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.`
1263	
1264	// executeBeadInstructionsAgentText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
1265	// inside <instructions> when running against the embedded Fizeau harness,
1266	// which has a minimal system prompt and needs more scaffolding to produce
1267	// reliable output. This version is more explicit about tools, process, and
1268	// stopping cleanly after the commit (to avoid the known post-commit runaway
1269	// failure mode).
1270	const executeBeadInstructionsAgentText = `You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.
1271	
1272	The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.
1273	
1274	## Step 0: size check (do this first)
1275	
1276	Before writing any code, decide whether this bead fits in one bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals it does not:
1277	
1278	- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
1279	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
1280	- Description explicitly names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
1281	- You estimate more than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
1282	
1283	If too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:
1284	
1285	1. Run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` (via bash) to file child beads, one per coherent slice — focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id.
1286	2. Run ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to wire the dependency edge.
1287	3. Run ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + ` so the decomposition is visible.
1288	4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` listing each child bead ID and the slice it covers, then stop.
1289	
1290	A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.
1291	
1292	If sized for one attempt, continue.
1293	
1294	## Process
1295	
1296	1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.
1297	
1298	2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.
1299	
1300	3. **Implement.** Use ` + "`edit`" + ` for targeted changes to existing files and ` + "`write`" + ` for brand-new files. Use ` + "`read`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: cat`" + `) to read files. Use ` + "`grep`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: rg`" + `) to search. Use ` + "`ls`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: ls`" + `) for directory listings.
1301	
1302	4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically ` + "`cargo test -p <crate>`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.
1303	
1304	5. **Commit exactly once.** Use ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` with the file paths you actually changed — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.
1305	
1306	6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.
1307	
1308	## If you cannot finish
1309	
1310	Writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit without a commit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.
1311	
1312	## Quality bar and the review step
1313	
1314	After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**
1315	
1316	If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, every BLOCKING finding in it is a concrete thing the previous attempt missed. Address each one before declaring the work complete — do not declare no_changes with blocking findings still unaddressed.
1317	
1318	## The bead contract overrides project defaults
1319	
1320	The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new files, write them. Conservative rules (YAGNI, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.
1321	
1322	## Constraints
1323	
1324	- Work only inside this execution worktree.
1325	- Keep ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + ` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
1326	- **Never run ` + "`ddx init`" + `** — it corrupts the bead queue.
1327	- Do not touch CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
1328	- Stage only the files you intentionally changed.`

input.old_string:
## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + ` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run ` + "`ddx init`" + `** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done
input.new_string:
## Investigation and report outputs

If the bead asks you to "output a report", "document findings", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/`" + ` (the per-attempt evidence directory under ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + `). **Never write reports to ` + "`/tmp`" + ` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines, which causes the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes so it lands in the merge.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + ` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run ` + "`ddx init`" + `** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.

If the bead asks you to "output a report", "document findings", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/`" + ` (the per-attempt evidence directory under ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + `). **Never write reports to ` + "`/tmp`" + ` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use that; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass.**

data.output:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
12	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
17	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
18	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
19	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
20	
21	### Key Components
22	
23	- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
24	  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
25	  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
26	  - `main.go` - Application entry point
27	- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
28	- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
29	  All plugins are project-local: `ddx install <name>` writes only under
30	  `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and
31	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Home-directory install paths
32	  (under `~/`) and the `ddx install --global` surface are retired.
33	  - `templates/` - Project templates
34	  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
35	  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
36	  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
37	  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
38	- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
39	- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts
40

14:## Architecture

193:## Persona System

193	## Persona System
194	
195	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
196	
197	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
198	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
199	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
200	
201	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
202	
203	<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->
204	<!-- Source: claude/system-prompts/focused.md -->
205	# System Instructions
206	
207	**Execute ONLY what is requested:**

input.old_string:
Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->
input.new_string:
Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

## Execution Evidence Convention

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.

<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->

data.text:
Committed as `6473f3fa`. AC satisfied:

1. **Prompt updated** — both `executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` and `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` now contain an "Investigation and report outputs" section directing agents to use `{{.AttemptDir}}` (resolved at runtime to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/`) and explicitly forbidding `/tmp`.
2. **CLAUDE.md** — added "Execution Evidence Convention" section documenting the rule.
3. **Test** — `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_path_test.go` verifies both prompt variants contain the directive and that `{{.AttemptDir}}` substitutes cleanly into a real `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` path.
